what_it_does: An interpretive ledger entry for the claude-ds file app/canvases/RegistryInspector.jsx, summarizing the component suite, behavior, conventions, concerns, and design intent as read from the file's content.
observations: Documents a React component suite for the Registry canvas: TypeInspector, SlotZones/SlotZone/AcceptsQuickEdit, VariableTags, ViChatScoped, ViStudioRail, AIRegistryPanel, and QuickAdder.
States that all editing mutates window.CV_REGISTRY through R.update/register/remove/children/lineage/resolve, with subscribe-driven re-render via a tick.
Records built-in type protection: name/desc inputs and slot/variable edits are disabled for isBuiltIn types; users are steered to Duplicate or + Variant.
Describes duplicate() as registering a copy with provenance:'user' and forkedFrom, and makeVariant() as registering a child with extends set to the parent id.
Notes SlotZone shows live candidatesForSlot matches as thumbnails and AcceptsQuickEdit toggles accepted layers using R.LAYERS / R.LAYER_INFO, excluding 'template'.

Describes runViAction as a structured prompt forcing ONE of three JSON intents (update / create-variant / answer), resolving the 'claude' provider via CV_AI.resolveProvider, and failing loudly if CV_AI is absent.
Records that Vi update/variant results are returned as PROPOSALS requiring an explicit Apply click before R.update/register runs.
Notes JSON parsing uses try/catch with a regex {…} fallback, and unparseable replies degrade to a user-facing 'try rewording' message.
States runStudioAction and QuickAdder delegate type drafting to window.CV_TYPES_VI.proposeType / suggestSlots, while AIRegistryPanel groups CV_AI items by AI.LAYERS.
Reports AIRegistryPanel uses inline styles with CSS-var fallbacks containing hex values (var(--accent-gold, #E0C010), var(--ink-2, #5b4628)).
Lists exports via Object.assign(window, {...}) at the end of the component file.
standouts: Forced three-intent JSON Vi dispatch (update / create-variant / answer) with explicit brand voice instructions and CV_AI provider resolution.
Proposal-then-apply gating: Vi never mutates the registry directly; the user confirms each proposed update or variant.
Built-in protection that prevents editing built-in types and offers fork (duplicate) or extend (variant) instead.
AIRegistryPanel projects the AI catalogue as a live, inspectable surface alongside the type registry.
Visual slot editing with live candidate-match thumbnails.
conventions: Follows the project's registry-as-truth pattern: UI as a projection of registries via subscribe/query.
Routes model access through CV_AI.resolveProvider and loud-fails when CV_AI is missing.
Uses window-global registration, React-via-globals, and the rs-* class namespace.
Treats provenance and lineage (forkedFrom, extends, lineage breadcrumbs) as first-class metadata.
Breaks the pure-token styling convention by using inline CSS-var fallbacks with concrete hex values in AIRegistryPanel.
concerns: runStudioAction is flagged with a 'For now' comment, indicating global Vi only proposes new Types and may be a stub.
AIRegistryPanel's inline hex fallbacks create a second home for token values, a drift risk the project warns against.
Multiple components subscribe independently to R/AI, which can multiply re-renders.
ViChatScoped.applyProposal compares x.proposal === p by reference, making proposal matching fragile if objects are cloned.
Variable/slot keys are free-text inputs without uniqueness or validation, so duplicate keys are possible.
Several actions silently no-op when window.CV_TYPES_VI is absent rather than failing loud.
notes: Interpretive summary of RegistryInspector.jsx: a visual, in-place registry-type editor with proposal-gated Vi assistance and a live AI-catalogue panel.
questions: Where is window.CV_TYPES_VI defined and what is its full contract for proposeType and suggestSlots?
Is the global studio action intended to grow into the same three-intent dispatch as the scoped chat?
Should AIRegistryPanel's inline hex fallbacks be removed in favor of pure tokens per the no-second-home rule?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — the described purpose (inspector + persistent Vi rail + quick adder with visual editing and ever-present Vi) aligns with the documented components and behaviors, with the noted 'for now' simplification in the global studio action.
apparent_themes: Registry-as-truth with the UI as its live projection
AI as a proposal engine gated by explicit user apply
Single-source AI access through CV_AI with loud failure
intention_signals: To make editing the type registry feel like direct, visual manipulation while keeping Vi as a controlled, proposal-making collaborator that never silently mutates the canonical registry.
novelty: Combines visual slot/variable editing, live candidate-match thumbnails, a forced three-intent JSON Vi dispatch, built-in fork/variant protection, and an inline AI-catalogue projection in one inspector suite.
contribution: Provides an interpretive record of the registry editing surface and its AI-assisted, proposal-gated interaction model, capturing component responsibilities, conventions, and known concerns for fusion.
summary_for_embedding: Interpretive ledger entry for the RegistryInspector.jsx component suite: a visual registry-type editor with slot/variable editing, built-in fork/variant protection, proposal-gated Vi actions via CV_AI, and an AIRegistryPanel projection.
intention_for_embedding: To document the RegistryInspector components as a direct-manipulation editing surface for CV_REGISTRY where Vi proposes changes that must be explicitly applied.
